creator: mfechner@FreeBSD.org
text:
Hi Boris,

Boris Samorodov schrieb:
> Now the port builds. But something weird with package(s) deletion:
> https://tinderbox.amd64.ipt.ru/tb/logs/6-bsam-exp/app_ldap-1.0.r6.log

I checked now everything and I'm sorry about it but I have no file
called /usr/local/etc/asterisk/dmsmgr.conf on my system.
But I found something on the website:
http://www.freebsdports.info/ports/net/asterisk.html
It seems to belongs to the asterisk port.
I found an entry in /usr/ports/net/asterisk/pkg-plist in line 48.

app_ldap only install two files:
/usr/local/lib/asterisk/modules/app_ldap.so
/usr/local/etc/asterisk/ldap.conf.sample

the .so file is the module and the second file is an example config.
If I do a make clean I get the following dependencies:
===>  Cleaning for asterisk-1.2.9.1_2
===>  Cleaning for openldap-client-2.3.27
===>  Cleaning for libiconv-1.9.2_2
===>  Cleaning for gmake-3.81_1
===>  Cleaning for mpg123-esound-0.59r_17
===>  Cleaning for libpri-1.2.3
===>  Cleaning for zaptel-1.0
===>  Cleaning for bison-1.75_2,1
===>  Cleaning for speex-1.0.5_1,1
===>  Cleaning for newt-0.51.0_3
===>  Cleaning for curl-7.15.5
===>  Cleaning for pwlib-1.10.1_2,1
===>  Cleaning for openh323-1.18.0_2
===>  Cleaning for unixODBC-2.2.11_1
===>  Cleaning for spandsp-0.0.2.p26
===>  Cleaning for libtool-1.5.22_2
===>  Cleaning for gettext-0.14.5_2
===>  Cleaning for esound-0.2.36_1
===>  Cleaning for m4-1.4.4
===>  Cleaning for libogg-1.1.3,3
===>  Cleaning for libslang-1.4.9
===>  Cleaning for popt-1.7_1
===>  Cleaning for autoconf-2.59_2
===>  Cleaning for expat-2.0.0_1
===>  Cleaning for tiff-3.8.2
===>  Cleaning for libaudiofile-0.2.6
===>  Cleaning for perl-5.8.8
===>  Cleaning for help2man-1.36.4_1
===>  Cleaning for jpeg-6b_4
===>  Cleaning for pkg-config-0.21
===>  Cleaning for p5-gettext-1.05_1
===>  Cleaning for app_ldap-1.0.r6_1

What script or package you use to verify the package, maybe I can
execute it here too?

I checked my package like it is described at:
http://www.freebsd.org/doc/en_US.ISO8859-1/books/porters-handbook/porting-testing.html

and all tests passed fine, after uninstall the two files are removed
successfully.

Bye
Matthias

-- 

"Programming today is a race between software engineers striving to
build bigger and better idiot-proof programs, and the universe trying to
produce bigger and better idiots. So far, the universe is winning." --
Rich Cook
